differentiable_at 𝕜 (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ n) x
source
theorem differentiable_within_at_pow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {s : set 𝕜} {n : ℕ} :
differentiable_within_at 𝕜 (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ n) s x
source
theorem differentiable_pow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {n : ℕ} :
differentiable 𝕜 (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ n)
source
theorem differentiable_on_pow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {s : set 𝕜} {n : ℕ} :
differentiable_on 𝕜 (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ n) s
source
theorem deriv_pow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {n : ℕ} :
deriv (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ n) x = (↑n) * x ^ (n - 1)
source
@[simp]
theorem deriv_pow' {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {n : ℕ} :
deriv (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ n) = λ (x : 𝕜), (↑n) * x ^ (n - 1)
source
theorem deriv_within_pow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {s : set 𝕜} {n : ℕ} (hxs : unique_diff_within_at 𝕜 s x) :
deriv_within (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ n) s x = (↑n) * x ^ (n - 1)
source
theorem iter_deriv_pow' {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {n k : ℕ} :
deriv^[k] (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ n) = λ (x : 𝕜), (↑∏ (i : ℕ) in finset.range k, (n - i)) * x ^ (n - k)
source
theorem iter_deriv_pow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {n k : ℕ} :
deriv^[k] (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ n) x = (↑∏ (i : ℕ) in finset.range k, (n - i)) * x ^ (n - k)
source
theorem has_deriv_within_at.pow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {s : set 𝕜} {c : 𝕜 → 𝕜} {c' : 𝕜} {n : ℕ} (hc : has_deriv_within_at c c' s x) :
has_deriv_within_at (λ (y : 𝕜), c y ^ n) (((↑n) * c x ^ (n - 1)) * c') s x
source
theorem has_deriv_at.pow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {c : 𝕜 → 𝕜} {c' : 𝕜} {n : ℕ} (hc : has_deriv_at c c' x) :
has_deriv_at (λ (y : 𝕜), c y ^ n) (((↑n) * c x ^ (n - 1)) * c') x
source
theorem differentiable_within_at.pow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {s : set 𝕜} {c : 𝕜 → 𝕜} {n : ℕ} (hc : differentiable_within_at 𝕜 c s x) :
differentiable_within_at 𝕜 (λ (x : 𝕜), c x ^ n) s x
source
@[simp]
theorem differentiable_at.pow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {c : 𝕜 → 𝕜} {n : ℕ} (hc : differentiable_at 𝕜 c x) :
differentiable_at 𝕜 (λ (x : 𝕜), c x ^ n) x
source
theorem differentiable_on.pow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {s : set 𝕜} {c : 𝕜 → 𝕜} {n : ℕ} (hc : differentiable_on 𝕜 c s) :
differentiable_on 𝕜 (λ (x : 𝕜), c x ^ n) s
source
@[simp]
theorem differentiable.pow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {c : 𝕜 → 𝕜} {n : ℕ} (hc : differentiable 𝕜 c) :
differentiable 𝕜 (λ (x : 𝕜), c x ^ n)
source
theorem deriv_within_pow' {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {s : set 𝕜} {c : 𝕜 → 𝕜} {n : ℕ} (hc : differentiable_within_at 𝕜 c s x) (hxs : unique_diff_within_at 𝕜 s x) :
deriv_within (λ (x : 𝕜), c x ^ n) s x = ((↑n) * c x ^ (n - 1)) * deriv_within c s x
source
@[simp]
theorem deriv_pow'' {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {c : 𝕜 → 𝕜} {n : ℕ} (hc : differentiable_at 𝕜 c x) :
deriv (λ (x : 𝕜), c x ^ n) x = ((↑n) * c x ^ (n - 1)) * deriv c x

Derivative of x ↦ x^m for m : ℤ #

source
theorem has_strict_deriv_at_fpow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} (m : ℤ) (hx : x ≠ 0) :
has_strict_deriv_at (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ m) ((↑m) * x ^ (m - 1)) x
source
theorem has_deriv_at_fpow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} (m : ℤ) (hx : x ≠ 0) :
has_deriv_at (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ m) ((↑m) * x ^ (m - 1)) x
source
theorem has_deriv_within_at_fpow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} (m : ℤ) (hx : x ≠ 0) (s : set 𝕜) :
has_deriv_within_at (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ m) ((↑m) * x ^ (m - 1)) s x
source
theorem differentiable_at_fpow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {m : ℤ} (hx : x ≠ 0) :
differentiable_at 𝕜 (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ m) x
source
theorem differentiable_within_at_fpow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {s : set 𝕜} {m : ℤ} (hx : x ≠ 0) :
differentiable_within_at 𝕜 (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ m) s x
source
theorem differentiable_on_fpow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {s : set 𝕜} {m : ℤ} (hs : 0 ∉ s) :
differentiable_on 𝕜 (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ m) s
source
theorem deriv_fpow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {m : ℤ} (hx : x ≠ 0) :
deriv (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ m) x = (↑m) * x ^ (m - 1)
source
theorem deriv_within_fpow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {s : set 𝕜} {m : ℤ} (hxs : unique_diff_within_at 𝕜 s x) (hx : x ≠ 0) :
deriv_within (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ m) s x = (↑m) * x ^ (m - 1)
source
theorem iter_deriv_fpow {𝕜 : Type u} [nondiscrete_normed_field 𝕜] {x : 𝕜} {m : ℤ} {k : ℕ} (hx : x ≠ 0) :
deriv^[k] (λ (x : 𝕜), x ^ m) x = (↑∏ (i : ℕ) in finset.range k, (m - ↑i)) * x ^ (m - ↑k)

Upper estimates on liminf and limsup #

source
theorem has_deriv_within_at.limsup_slope_le {f : ℝ → ℝ} {f' : ℝ} {s : set ℝ} {x r : ℝ} (hf : has_deriv_within_at f f' s x) (hr : f' < r) :
∀ᶠ (z : ℝ) in 𝓝[s \ {x}] x, (z - x)⁻¹ * (f z - f x) < r
source
theorem has_deriv_within_at.limsup_slope_le' {f : ℝ → ℝ} {f' : ℝ} {s : set ℝ} {x r : ℝ} (hf : has_deriv_within_at f f' s x) (hs : x ∉ s) (hr : f' < r) :
∀ᶠ (z : ℝ) in 𝓝[s] x, (z - x)⁻¹ * (f z - f x) < r
source
theorem has_deriv_within_at.liminf_right_slope_le {f : ℝ → ℝ} {f' x r : ℝ} (hf : has_deriv_within_at f f' (set.Ici x) x) (hr : f' < r) :
∃ᶠ (z : ℝ) in 𝓝[set.Ioi x] x, (z - x)⁻¹ * (f z - f x) < r
source
theorem has_deriv_within_at.limsup_norm_slope_le {E : Type u} [normed_group E] [normed_space ℝ E] {f : ℝ → E} {f' : E} {s : set ℝ} {x r : ℝ} (hf : has_deriv_within_at f f' s x) (hr : ∥f'∥ < r) :
∀ᶠ (z : ℝ) in 𝓝[s] x, ∥z - x∥⁻¹ * ∥f z - f x∥ < r

If f has derivative f' within s at x, then for any r > ∥f'∥ the ratio ∥f z - f x∥ / ∥z - x∥ is less than r in some neighborhood of x within s. In other words, the limit superior of this ratio as z tends to x along s is less than or equal to ∥f'∥.

source
theorem has_deriv_within_at.limsup_slope_norm_le {E : Type u} [normed_group E] [normed_space ℝ E] {f : ℝ → E} {f' : E} {s : set ℝ} {x r : ℝ} (hf : has_deriv_within_at f f' s x) (hr : ∥f'∥ < r) :
∀ᶠ (z : ℝ) in 𝓝[s] x, ∥z - x∥⁻¹ * (∥f z∥ - ∥f x∥) < r

If f has derivative f' within s at x, then for any r > ∥f'∥ the ratio (∥f z∥ - ∥f x∥) / ∥z - x∥ is less than r in some neighborhood of x within s. In other words, the limit superior of this ratio as z tends to x along s is less than or equal to ∥f'∥.

This lemma is a weaker version of has_deriv_within_at.limsup_norm_slope_le where ∥f z∥ - ∥f x∥ is replaced by ∥f z - f x∥.

source
theorem has_deriv_within_at.liminf_right_norm_slope_le {E : Type u} [normed_group E] [normed_space ℝ E] {f : ℝ → E} {f' : E} {x r : ℝ} (hf : has_deriv_within_at f f' (set.Ici x) x) (hr : ∥f'∥ < r) :
∃ᶠ (z : ℝ) in 𝓝[set.Ioi x] x, ∥z - x∥⁻¹ * ∥f z - f x∥ < r

If f has derivative f' within (x, +∞) at x, then for any r > ∥f'∥ the ratio ∥f z - f x∥ / ∥z - x∥ is frequently less than r as z → x+0. In other words, the limit inferior of this ratio as z tends to x+0 is less than or equal to ∥f'∥. See also has_deriv_within_at.limsup_norm_slope_le for a stronger version using limit superior and any set s.

source
theorem has_deriv_within_at.liminf_right_slope_norm_le {E : Type u} [normed_group E] [normed_space ℝ E] {f : ℝ → E} {f' : E} {x r : ℝ} (hf : has_deriv_within_at f f' (set.Ici x) x) (hr : ∥f'∥ < r) :
∃ᶠ (z : ℝ) in 𝓝[set.Ioi x] x, (z - x)⁻¹ * (∥f z∥ - ∥f x∥) < r

If f has derivative f' within (x, +∞) at x, then for any r > ∥f'∥ the ratio (∥f z∥ - ∥f x∥) / (z - x) is frequently less than r as z → x+0. In other words, the limit inferior of this ratio as z tends to x+0 is less than or equal to ∥f'∥.

See also

  • has_deriv_within_at.limsup_norm_slope_le for a stronger version using limit superior and any set s;
  • has_deriv_within_at.liminf_right_norm_slope_le for a stronger version using ∥f z - f x∥ instead of ∥f z∥ - ∥f x∥.
